rulings.Rulings: Continued by judicial officer to 11/17/2021 be heard after other petition.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERTA PERSON FAMILY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 23, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Settlement Of Account And Approval Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursement Of Costs For 2018 And 2019
rulings.Rulings: Continued by judicial officer to 11/03/2021. Issue to be addressed prior to the hearing: The Judicial Officer requests an explanation of whether some portion of the attorney fees were a benefit to the co-trustees individually not to the trust. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The Petition for Settlement of Account and Approval of Attorney Fees and Reimbursement of Costs for 2018 and 2019 issues: a. On Schedule C: Disbursements 7/16/2018 $200.00 withdrawal Dessie Silva is not explained. b. On Schedule C: Disbursements 3/5/2019 $7,781.24 Brian McCaffrey attorney fees is not explained. c. On Schedule C: Disbursements 3/18/2019 withdrawal of $2,000.00 is not explained. d. On Schedule C: Disbursements 4/9/2019 transfer of $2,000.00 is not explained. e. Attorney requests $10,010.00 in attorney fees and $504.74 in costs for 2018 be approved and $7,781.25 from trust funds and the successor f. Attorney requests $30,270.50 in fees and $1,939.38 in costs for 2019 be approved and successor trustee be authorized to pay. g. It appears the attorney spent approximately 50.7 hours @ $385.00 per hour on the accounts which appears extraordinarily high based on the simplicity of the accounts. h. Attorney is billing 4 hours @ his attorney rate of $385.00 per hour to travel to get signatures. 2) Remaining Issues a. For the 2013 account the court did not approve $1,070.00 for credit card payment described as "miscellaneous." b. For the 2016-2017 account there were $337.19 in unexplained expenditures and $4,358.00 in unexplained withdrawals. c. Petitioner Jerome Black requested: i. Successor trustees Dessie Silva and Frank Lewis be personally liable for surcharge and interest at the legal rate on the surcharge according to proof; ii. Successor trustees Dessie Silva and Frank Lewis be ordered to pay Petitioner Jerome Black for legal fees and costs incurred in this action- the amount awarded to be determined according to proof presented at trial. [Appears to be nothing filed.] d. Examiner does not have a proposed order for the remaining issues for Jerome Black's petition. [See LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/2/2021. 2nd continuance same issues. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/18/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JESSE R BARBER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/17/21. (No earlier availability.) Schedule C has some incomplete entries (payee or description) and the date of any order authorizing fees must be set forth. [CRC 7.575(e)(6)(C).] Was one of the two identical entries on 12/23/19 for Clothing a duplicate? Living expenses paid to the conservator must clearly identify what they were for (not just an invoice number) or who the original payee was (not just "clothing"). The original bank statements were not yet in the Confidential file. The photocopy shows just pages 1 and 3 of the WFB statement for 6/7/21 and pages 1 and 3 of the Ameritrade statement ending 5/31/21. There is no reconciliation shown in the accounting for the difference in the beginning or ending balances shown on p. 1 of the WFB statement and in the accounting. The number of shares of most of the funds held in the TD Ameritrade account at the end of the accounting period do not appear to be correct, if Schedule G is correct. The boxing sports memorabilia should be shown in detail as shown in the I&A. (The formatting for part of the Gains schedule was difficult to read! It would also have been helpful if the informational schedule (G) had shown the total of the carry values, as funds purchased separately were all sold together.) Courtesy copies of any responsive pleadings must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/5/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. Parties have not complied with multiple judicial case management orders. Court to determine whether to sanction the parties/attorneys. 2) Parties should be prepared to cite code sections and explain the legal authority for settling without a GAL being appointed or involved in protecting the minor's interest. GAL was appointed after the settlement was reached. 3) Attorney Amanda Ebey has not provided the required courtesy copy of the Petition For Approval of Settlement Agreement, related documents or the required proposed order. [LRSF 14.7]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ILENE BRETHOLZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Determination Of Persons Entitled To Estate Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/16/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: An amended petition was filed on 9/22/21 (but not actually E-filed, i.e., available for viewing, until 9/28/21), which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. Notice may not be mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] Additionally, courtesy copies of the amended petition and proof of service have not yet been received by the assigned Examiner as of 10/1/21. Courtesy copies must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/5/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CEASAR A. RIBEIRO 2003 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Reformation Or In The Alternative Termination Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/23/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: The Court's register of actions indicates that there are documents in the E-filing queue. If an amended petition is pending filing, notice must be given of the amended petition after it is actually E-filed (available for viewing). Notice may not be given for a pleading until after that pleading is filed, and trusts require 30 days' notice. [LRSF 14.14 A. and CRC 7.53(a).] Courtesy copies of whatever is eventually actually E-filed, including supporting or associated papers (proof of service and a proposed order) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/12/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF A. NATIVIDAD PARAS NISCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. Issues to be addressed: 1) Still no proposed order has been submitted (or received by this Examiner). [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.60 E.] 2) Only a copy of the will is attached to the petition because the original is apparently in limbo in the Philippines. 3) The will was signed by the testator and the witnesses on 2/3/2020, but the notary acknowledgment was dated 2/3/2019. 4) Petitioner alleges in paragraph 8 of her supplement filed on 8/19/21 that attorney Lorna Imelda M. Suarez "is serving as the Executor and Administrator" in the Philippines. Nothing has been filed to confirm that the will was admitted in the Philippines and that Ms. Suarez was appointed there. 5) If the Court sees fit to appoint Petitioner as Administrator with Will Annexed without the original will or a certified copy of an order admitting the will in the domiciliary proceeding, does the Court accept Petitioner's allegation that she is actually a permanent resident of California, only temporarily residing in Oregon, so that no bond should be required?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH RICHARD PIERCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/17/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Item 2b of the petition does not show the name of the proposed personal representative. Item 3a of the petition is incomplete. Item 3e(3) of the petition must be checked if the decedent died intestate. Item 5b of the petition is incomplete. These issues must be addressed in a supplement, signed and verified by the petitioner. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. (Item 7 of the petition does not need to be completed when issue survived.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/8/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/26/2021 first available date. Issue: Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/12/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
